title: Pyrenaria maculatoclada
taxonomy: Pyrenaria maculatoclada belongs to the family Theaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to Asia, specifically in the Himalayan region and parts of China. The plant is also known as an evergreen tree or shrub with oval-shaped leaves. Its classification places it within the genus Pyrenaria, which is part of the larger family of plants known as Theaceae. Further classification includes its order (Ericales), class (Magnoliopsida), and kingdom (Plantae).

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal benefits.
